reforms.description: The government increased the training and adjustment uses of unemployment insurance funds to C$ 2.2 billion in 1993, significantly enhancing support for active labour-market policies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing support for active labour-market policies is expected to improve workforce skills and employability, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increase was executed in 1993.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in funding for training and adjustment is significant but not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Furthermore, the training and adjustment uses of UI funds were increased to C$ 2.2 billion in 1993, more than four times the amount budgeted in 1990.

reforms.description: The government announced its intention to streamline delivery of and access to R&D programmes and services, enhancing support for Canadian corporations undertaking R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining access to R&D programmes is expected to facilitate innovation and improve the efficiency of R&D investments by corporations.
reforms.status_evidence: The announcement was made in 1993 and has been executed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The intention to streamline R&D programmes is significant but does not represent a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: At the same time, it announced its intention to streamline delivery of and access to R&D programmes and services.
